test_cases.title: User successfully logs in with valid standard credentials
preconditions: User is on the login page

steps.action: Navigate to the login page
steps.expected_result: Login page loads successfully, displaying 'Email' and 'Password' input fields and a 'Login' button.

steps.action: Enter the valid email address into the 'Email' field
steps.expected_result: The 'Email' field is populated with 'valid.user@example.com'.

steps.action: Enter the valid password into the 'Password' field
steps.expected_result: The 'Password' field is populated with masked characters, and the 'Login' button becomes enabled.

steps.action: Click the 'Login' button
steps.expected_result: User is redirected to the dashboard/home page. A welcome message (e.g., 'Welcome, valid.user!') is displayed, and the user's profile icon/name is visible.

steps.action: Verify user session establishment
steps.expected_result: The URL matches the expected post-login page (e.g., https://yourdomain.com/dashboard), and a 'Logout' option is clearly visible, confirming session establishment.

test_cases.title: User successfully logs in with a password containing various special characters
preconditions: User is on the login page

steps.action: Navigate to the login page
steps.expected_result: Login page loads successfully.

steps.action: Enter the valid email address into the 'Email' field
steps.expected_result: The 'Email' field is populated.

steps.action: Enter the password 'ComplexP@ssw0rd!#$%' into the 'Password' field
steps.expected_result: The 'Password' field is populated with masked characters, and the 'Login' button becomes enabled.

steps.action: Click the 'Login' button
steps.expected_result: User is successfully logged in and redirected to the dashboard/home page, indicating correct handling of special characters in passwords.

test_cases.title: Attempt login with valid email but invalid password
preconditions: User is on the login page

steps.action: Navigate to the login page
steps.expected_result: Login page loads successfully.

steps.action: Enter the valid email address into the 'Email' field
steps.expected_result: The 'Email' field is populated.

steps.action: Enter an incorrect password into the 'Password' field
steps.expected_result: The 'Password' field is populated with masked characters, and the 'Login' button becomes enabled.

steps.action: Click the 'Login' button
steps.expected_result: An appropriate error message is displayed (e.g., 'Invalid email or password' or 'Incorrect password. Please try again.'). The user remains on the login page and is NOT logged in.

test_cases.title: Attempt login with a non-existent email address
preconditions: User is on the login page

steps.action: Navigate to the login page
steps.expected_result: Login page loads successfully.

steps.action: Enter a non-existent email address into the 'Email' field
steps.expected_result: The 'Email' field is populated.

steps.action: Enter a dummy password into the 'Password' field
steps.expected_result: The 'Password' field is populated with masked characters, and the 'Login' button becomes enabled.

steps.action: Click the 'Login' button
steps.expected_result: An appropriate error message is displayed (e.g., 'Invalid email or password' or 'Account not found.'). The user remains on the login page and is NOT logged in. The error message should ideally be generic to prevent account enumeration.

test_cases.title: Account lockout triggered after 5 consecutive failed login attempts
preconditions: User is on the login page

steps.action: Navigate to the login page
steps.expected_result: Login page loads successfully.

steps.action: Attempt to log in for the 1st time with correct email and incorrect password
steps.expected_result: An 'Invalid credentials' or 'Incorrect password' error message is displayed. User remains on login page.

steps.action: Repeat login attempt with incorrect password for the 2nd time
steps.expected_result: An 'Invalid credentials' or 'Incorrect password' error message is displayed. User remains on login page.

steps.action: Repeat login attempt with incorrect password for the 3rd time
steps.expected_result: An 'Invalid credentials' or 'Incorrect password' error message is displayed. User remains on login page.

steps.action: Repeat login attempt with incorrect password for the 4th time
steps.expected_result: An 'Invalid credentials' or 'Incorrect password' error message is displayed. User remains on login page.

steps.action: Attempt to log in for the 5th time with correct email and incorrect password
steps.expected_result: A specific error message indicating account lockout is displayed (e.g., 'Your account has been locked due to too many failed login attempts. Please try again in X minutes' or 'Please contact support.'). User remains on login page and is not logged in.

steps.action: Attempt to log in with correct credentials for the locked account
steps.expected_result: The account remains locked, and the lockout error message is still displayed. User is NOT logged in, confirming the lockout mechanism.

test_cases.title: Successful login with a valid email address at maximum practical length
preconditions: User is on the login page

steps.action: Navigate to the login page
steps.expected_result: Login page loads successfully.

steps.action: Enter the very long valid email address into the 'Email' field
steps.expected_result: The 'Email' field accepts and displays the entire long email address without truncation or error.

steps.action: Enter the corresponding password into the 'Password' field
steps.expected_result: The 'Password' field is populated with masked characters, and the 'Login' button becomes enabled.

steps.action: Click the 'Login' button
steps.expected_result: User is successfully logged in and redirected to the dashboard/home page, verifying support for long email addresses (AC1).

test_cases.title: Successful login with a password meeting the minimum length requirement
preconditions: User is on the login page
System has a minimum password length requirement (e.g., 8 characters)
User 'minlen@example.com' exists with password 'MinPass8!' (8 characters long)

steps.action: Navigate to the login page
steps.expected_result: Login page loads successfully.

steps.action: Enter the valid email address into the 'Email' field
steps.expected_result: The 'Email' field is populated.

steps.action: Enter the 8-character password 'MinPass8!' into the 'Password' field
steps.expected_result: The 'Password' field is populated with masked characters, and the 'Login' button becomes enabled.

steps.action: Click the 'Login' button
steps.expected_result: User is successfully logged in and redirected to the dashboard/home page, confirming that passwords at the minimum length boundary are accepted (AC1).

test_cases.title: UI validation for empty fields and invalid email format
preconditions: User is on the login page

steps.action: Navigate to the login page
steps.expected_result: Login page loads successfully with 'Email' and 'Password' fields, and a 'Login' button. The 'Login' button should initially be disabled or inactive if fields are empty.

steps.action: Click the 'Login' button without entering any credentials
steps.expected_result: Client-side validation errors appear for both 'Email' and 'Password' fields (e.g., 'Email is required', 'Password is required'). The 'Login' button remains disabled/inactive, and no network request is sent.

steps.action: Enter an invalid email format into the 'Email' field and a valid password, then click 'Login'
steps.expected_result: A client-side validation error appears for the 'Email' field (e.g., 'Please enter a valid email address'). The 'Login' button may become enabled but the submission should be blocked or return an error after server validation if client-side validation is bypassed. User remains on login page.

steps.action: Enter a valid email and an empty password, then click 'Login'
steps.expected_result: A client-side validation error appears for the 'Password' field (e.g., 'Password is required'). The 'Login' button may become enabled but submission should be blocked. User remains on login page.

test_cases.title: Attempt SQL injection in the email field
preconditions: User is on the login page

steps.action: Navigate to the login page
steps.expected_result: Login page loads successfully.

steps.action: Enter a known SQL Injection payload into the 'Email' field and a dummy password
steps.expected_result: The 'Email' field accepts the input. The 'Login' button becomes enabled.

steps.action: Click the 'Login' button
steps.expected_result: The system should NOT log in the user as 'admin' or any other user. An appropriate error message should be displayed (e.g., 'Invalid email or password' or 'Your input could not be processed.'). The user remains on the login page. There should be no server errors or unexpected behavior in logs.

test_cases.title: Attempt Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) in the password field
preconditions: User is on the login page

steps.action: Navigate to the login page
steps.expected_result: Login page loads successfully.

steps.action: Enter a valid email and an XSS payload into the 'Password' field
steps.expected_result: The 'Password' field accepts the input, likely masked. The 'Login' button becomes enabled.

steps.action: Click the 'Login' button
steps.expected_result: The XSS script should NOT execute (no alert box or unexpected page modification). An appropriate error message (e.g., 'Invalid email or password') should be displayed. The user remains on the login page and is NOT logged in. Server logs should not show errors related to script execution.
